posts.text:

This article on “Philanthrocapitalism” was really eye-opening for me

posts.text:
“Giving to charity is a prime opportunity for CEOs to be seen to be doing good without having to sacrifice their commitment to making profit at any social cost.”

“Charitable activity permits CEOs to be philanthropic rather than economically progressive or politically democratic.”

posts.text: The primary thing Bezos gets in return for these donations is reputational boost. Philanthropy is a PR strategy.
posts.quote: The primary thing Bezos gets in return for these donations is reputational boost. Philanthropy is a PR strategy.

posts.text:
Bezos is one of the biggest funders of climate orgs but behaves like someone who works for the oil and gas industry. Which makes one wonder what he gets in return for all of those donations. 

 "according to the Bezos Earth Fund, AI could be humanity’s best hope for curbing climate change"

posts.text:
Big Oil is no longer trying to convince us they want to preserve a safe & stable climate

Now, they're focused on convincing us that the world is only safe & stable if they're in charge

That's the thesis of a new @cleancreatives.bsky.social report analyzing 2000 oil ads, exclusively in HEATED today

posts.text: Jesus. Paying a French company a billion dollars *not* to build a wind farm.
posts.quote: Jesus. Paying a French company a billion dollars *not* to build a wind farm.

posts.text: "The oil industry is spending $7 billion a year telling you oil is inevitable not because it’s true, but because it desperately needs everyone to believe it."
posts.quote: "The oil industry is spending $7 billion a year telling you oil is inevitable not because it’s true, but because it desperately needs everyone to believe it."

posts.text: "But it’s important to remember: The oil industry is spending $7 billion a year telling you oil is inevitable not because it’s true, but because it desperately needs everyone to believe it. If everyone does not believe this, they are screwed."
posts.quote: "But it’s important to remember: The oil industry is spending $7 billion a year telling you oil is inevitable not because it’s true, but because it desperately needs everyone to believe it. If everyone does not believe this, they are screwed."

posts.text:
Great story on new @cleancreatives.bsky.social research showing that the era of fossil fuel greenwashing is largely over.

Ever since the 2022 Ukraine invasion oil and gas companies have doubled down on extraction, and are using ad and PR campaigns to try to crush their opposition not embrace them.

posts.text: How fossil fuel ads manipulate us. On today’s podcast, we watch and analyze fossil fuel ads.

posts.text: Fossil fuel propaganda is evolving. A new analysis of nearly 2,000 fossil fuel ads finds Big Oil has moved from green promises to insisting oil and gas are inevitable. (They're not).
posts.quote: Fossil fuel propaganda is evolving. A new analysis of nearly 2,000 fossil fuel ads finds Big Oil has moved from green promises to insisting oil and gas are inevitable. (They're not).

posts.text: Can a billionaire fix California?. Tom Steyer argues he's not a regular billionaire. He's a cool billionaire.

posts.text: NBC's top climate reporter resigns. In an exclusive interview, veteran NBC meteorologist Chase Cain opens up about burnout, suppression, and why he's going independent.
posts.quote: NBC's top climate reporter resigns. In an exclusive interview, veteran NBC meteorologist Chase Cain opens up about burnout, suppression, and why he's going independent.

posts.text: Sam Alito has an oil money problem. Nearly 30 percent of Alito’s individual stock portfolio is directly tied to fossil fuels.
posts.quote: Sam Alito has an oil money problem. Nearly 30 percent of Alito’s individual stock portfolio is directly tied to fossil fuels.

posts.text: Trump is wiping out all climate regulation. Big Oil may regret it.  (by Julia Kane). For decades, oil majors fought climate regulation. Now, they’re afraid Trump’s extreme rollbacks could leave them on the hook to pay for what they’ve done.
posts.quote: Trump is wiping out all climate regulation. Big Oil may regret it.  (by Julia Kane). For decades, oil majors fought climate regulation. Now, they’re afraid Trump’s extreme rollbacks could leave them on the hook to pay for what they’ve done.

posts.text: Actually, I do know how to do this. In retrospect, paying attention to polluters may be one of the best ways to understand what’s currently happening in the United States.
posts.quote: Actually, I do know how to do this. In retrospect, paying attention to polluters may be one of the best ways to understand what’s currently happening in the United States.
